summary.intent: Implement task #205 — the framework-agnostic 'Group' utility component core for the ars-ui project, delivering a stateless attribute mapper with a GroupContext struct for disabled/invalid/read_only propagation to descendants.
summary.outcome: Full implementation delivered: group.rs (GroupRole, Props, GroupContext, Part, Api, ConnectApi + 33 unit tests + 5 snapshots), spec corrections, spec-conformance test, and module registration. All 20 cargo xci gates passed, 100% line/region/function coverage, 0 missed mutations, committed and pushed with PR closing #205.

repo: CLAUDE.md designates spec as the authoritative contract — when issue bullets contradict the spec, the spec wins unconditionally.

Post-implementation-audit is mandatory per CLAUDE.md §7: three phases — (a) spec/impl drift, (b) ≥2 iterative 'anything else?' rounds until two consecutive clean rounds, (c) test coverage + mutation audit.
Spec edits must land in the same PR as the implementation per the 'no spec drift' rule.
Secondary context structs use descriptive non-prefixed names per foundation 08 §8.4 — e.g., GroupContext not Context, so consumer writes group::GroupContext.
Coverage threshold for ars-components is 90/90 (line/region); enforced via cargo llvm-cov.
Snapshot tests use cargo insta; reviewed snapshots earn the PR label 'snapshot-reviewed'; orphan check runs with --unreferenced=reject.
Spec-conformance anatomy tests live in crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/utility.rs and must be added for every new component.
cargo doc errors are pre-existing tech debt and are not part of xci/CI gates — only clippy warnings are hard-failed.
cargo mutants is used for mutation testing; unviable mutations (const fn / builder-return-self) are a free correctness signal, not a coverage gap.
Issues must be moved to 'In Progress' on the GitHub project board at implementation start via gh CLI.

code.finding: Canonical template for stateless utility components — same Props/Part/Api/ConnectApi/tests structure used for Group.
Reference pattern for a secondary context struct (FieldContext) — Group's GroupContext follows this shape.
Direction::as_html_attr — used to emit the dir attribute in root_attrs.
Spec-conformance anatomy tests for all utility components live here; a group_anatomy_matches_spec test was missing and had to be added during the post-audit.

Had two internal contradictions: §3.1 prose said state attrs are omitted for role=Presentation (wrong per WAI-ARIA 1.2 §5.4), and the Api struct showed borrowed &'a Props but impl correctly uses owned Props. Both fixed in the same PR.

workflow: cargo xci is the full pre-PR gate (20 checks); must pass with exit code 0 before committing.
TDD ordering: tests written in the same file as implementation (not a separate test file) per workspace convention.
cargo insta snapshot workflow: first test run generates .snap.new files → review them manually → cargo insta accept → commit the .snap files.
cargo mutants -p <crate> -f <file> scopes mutation testing to a single file, keeping runtime practical.
cargo xtask spec validate checks spec frontmatter for all 340 spec files; cargo test -p xtask --test spec_corpus_compile_snippets verifies code examples in specs actually compile.
When cargo xci runs in background, its output file stays empty until completion because the recipe pipes through tail -50 — check task status rather than file size to gauge progress.
wasm-coverage stage for ars-dioxus is the slowest xci gate (~25-30 min); plan accordingly.

friction: cargo xci took ~30+ minutes due to wasm-coverage stage compiling ars-dioxus with nightly wasm32 instrument-coverage — the output file appeared empty throughout, making progress opaque.
GitHub issue #205 contained two contradicting test bullets (label/label_id props) that don't exist in the spec, requiring explicit resolution and user confirmation before proceeding.
Internal spec contradiction between §1.2 code example and §3.1 prose on state attribute emission for role=Presentation required research into WAI-ARIA 1.2 §5.4 to resolve confidently.
Post-implementation audit surfaced 7 spec drift issues (including a medium-high severity Api<'a> lifetime mismatch), all requiring spec rewrites in the same PR.
Snapshot tests always fail on first run by design, requiring a separate cargo insta accept step that could surprise contributors unfamiliar with insta.
Pre-existing doc warnings in the codebase triggered a false alarm during cargo doc check, requiring investigation to confirm they predated this PR.
open_items: Leptos adapter: ars-leptos Group component — separate task on the project board.
Dioxus adapter: ars-dioxus Group component — separate task on the project board.
Wiring GroupContext consumption into existing child components (Button, TextField, etc.) — blocked on adapter components existing first; separate cross-component tasks.
Pre-existing cargo doc warnings in the codebase (not in group.rs) — tech debt unrelated to #205, no issue filed.
